name: Devil's Food with Fudge Frosting
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons half-and-half, milk, or evaporated milk for frosting
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Grease two 8-inch layer cake pans well and dust them generously with cocoa powder (this gives the cake layers a rich brown finish).
3. Sift the flour, cocoa powder, baking soda, and salt onto a piece of waxed paper and set it aside.
4. Cream the butter until fluffy, then add the sugar and vanilla, continuing to beat until the mixture is silvery and light.
5. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then alternately with the sifted dry ingredients and boiling water, beginning and ending with the dry ingredients. Be sure to beat well after each addition.
6. Divide the batter equally between the two pans, smoothing the tops.
7. Bake the layers uncovered for 30 to 35 minutes, or until the layers feel spongy to the touch and begin to pull away from the sides of the pans.
8. Cool the layers upright in their pans on wire racks for 10 minutes, then carefully loosen them around the edges with a spatula and turn them onto wire racks. Cool completely.
9. For the frosting: Cream the butter until light, then gradually add the confectioners' sugar, beating continuously. Beat in the melted chocolate and vanilla, then add just enough half-and-half, milk, or evaporated milk to achieve a good consistency for spreading.
10. Sandwich the two cake layers together with frosting, then frost the top and sides of the cake. Swirl the frosting into peaks and valleys.
